audit_scope: Static, no-cost audit of the exact official EnergyEvals archive pinned at commit 90cc6165d514cdc37c3a3df8de95a9839a1f3a08. No model, search, market-data, tariff, weather, docket, or other live/paid API was called.
paper_release_timing: The commit is 4 hours 30 minutes 40 seconds after immutable arXiv v1 and is treated as post-v1 release evidence, not assumed manuscript-time identity.

important_limits: The CSV contains prompts and coarse labels only; it has no ground truth, attributes, tolerances, source snapshots, evidence locators, author/reviewer lineage, or valid-time record.
Its 188 rows and category counts do not match the paper's 243-task 107/86/50 corpus or Appendix A.13's 212-question statement.
IDs are non-contiguous and extend to 281, so row subtraction alone cannot reconstruct the paper form or explain attrition.

traces.shape: 12 model directories x 36 question IDs = 432 JSON traces

query_identity.explanation: All 36 nonmatches are twelve traces each for q115, q190, and q194. q115 differs by whitespace; q190 and q194 omit two substantive current-CSV instructions about excluding classes without demand charges and including adjustment rates.

important_limits: The twelve released model names do not reproduce the paper's seven-model panel; only Claude Sonnet 4.6 overlaps by displayed family/version.
The release has 36 traced questions, not the paper's stated 30-question released subset.

The trace metadata records provider/model labels, model parameters, tool names, timestamps, arguments, outputs, tokens, and latency, but not immutable provider responses, API/search index snapshots, response content hashes, dataset/document versions, ground truth, judge outputs, or paper-table row identity.

implementation_findings.finding: README instructs users to run scripts/run_eval.py with configs/eval_config.yaml, but neither path exists in the archive; no grading implementation or raw judge report is released.

The released system prompt explicitly requires a source for every claim, figure, or data point, so source-citation performance is scaffold-conditioned rather than emergent. This prompt is richer than the three-line prompt printed in paper Appendix A.8.

The shipped example uses openai/gpt-4o-mini, medium effort, one trial, shuffle=true, and 50 iterations; it is not a manifest for the seven paper configurations, which are described as low reasoning effort and temperature zero.

The database tool is an optional external package and no RAG tool is shipped, although both are central paper tool categories. The released traces used an unshipped database tool, so the archived repository alone cannot recreate their environment.

Search and page retrieval use mutable Exa results/cache/livecrawl with URLs and optional publication dates but no retrieval receipt containing provider index version, response hash, or archived content identity.

GridStatus calls a live commercial API and writes response rows to transient CSV, but does not preserve response headers, API/database snapshot, payload hash, or source valid time beyond fields that happen to occur in returned data.

Docker code execution has useful read-only/resource controls, but network defaults to unrestricted bridge; the firewall is opt-in. The entire repository data directory is mounted read-only, so private evaluation materials would need a separate firewall if present.

The optimization uses perfect foresight, a fixed 50% initial/terminal state, continuous charge/discharge variables, IPOPT, and accepts feasible or locallyOptimal termination. It supplies one modeling policy and witness, not a validated professional decision or guaranteed globally optimal reference.

The current runner supports seeded shuffle and multiple trials but the shipped configuration requests one. A fresh provider object is created per question and tools are mutable external services; no synchronized evidence snapshot or environment receipt makes repeated/model comparisons exposure-equivalent.

claim_boundary: This audit establishes inspectable release structure, discrepancies, and implementation behavior only. It does not validate the paper's scores, expert authority, task representativeness, live-source correctness, quantitative references, judge accuracy, treatment effects, professional utility, production fitness, safety, or readiness.
